topics.title: Europe heatwave pushes east
topics.summary: Dangerous heat has shifted into central, eastern and southern Europe, with some cities forecast above 40°C and officials opening cooling centres and issuing red alerts.
topics.discussionPrompt: When extreme heat starts affecting daily routines, do you think cities should redesign around it, or mostly focus on emergency warnings?

topics.title: Brazil edges Japan at World Cup
topics.summary: Gabriel Martinelli scored in stoppage time as Brazil beat Japan 2-1 in a World Cup knockout match, ending Japan’s hopes of a first knockout win.
topics.discussionPrompt: Do late-game goals make soccer more exciting, or do they feel unfair after one team has defended well for so long?

topics.title: Wimbledon opens with surprises
topics.summary: Wimbledon’s first day brought tough matches and upsets, with Jannik Sinner surviving a five-set test and Naomi Osaka advancing in straight sets.
topics.discussionPrompt: What makes a tournament more fun to follow: seeing favourites cruise through, or watching early upsets shake everything up?

topics.title: Canada backs Greenland critical minerals
topics.summary: Canada announced C$7 million in grants for a Greenland molybdenum project, supporting a metal used in aerospace, energy and defence industries.
topics.discussionPrompt: Critical minerals sound boring until they affect technology and security. Should countries invest more in supply chains close to home?
